creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 concludes on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco with keynotes, main-stage programming, and startup showcase. The livestream covers the final day of this industry conference.

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 livestream held July 1, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming covering AI engineering topics throughout the day.

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 took place on June 30, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco, featuring keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day. The event was streamed online by AI Engineer, covering one of the largest gatherings in AI engineering.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack context and memory continuity across interactions, making them ineffective despite individual capability. He uses an analogy of a genius engineer who can only see a tiny corner of a codebase and forgets everything between interactions to illustrate the problem that needs solving.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of software development results while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) accounts for 90%. The video frames agentic engineering as the successor to vibe coding and emphasizes context engineering and orchestration skills as critical for 2026.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use agentic loops that automatically prompt and iterate on model outputs rather than direct prompting. The approach involves an orchestrator spawning parallel worker agents and requires observability systems to manage cost and mitigate hallucinations. Cole Medin built a TypeScript dashboard that implements this with Pi running on Kimi, storing runs in a database.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness for orchestrating multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi, others) together under unified governance. It enables workflows where different models handle different tasks like implementation and code review, with human-in-the-loop controls and cross-device session persistence.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden withdrawal of Claude Fable 5 three days after launch, allegedly due to a US government export-control directive regarding a claimed jailbreak. The video covers the regulatory action, reasons for the overnight removal, implications for developers, and Medin's pre-removal benchmarking of the model against Claude Opus 4.8.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a new secret project related to OpenClaw Creator, featuring a GitHub repository with skills for setting up Crabbox and a codebase harness. The content promotes the AI Builder Club community and Superdesign platform.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer, a tool for building and setting up AI agents. The content includes a GitHub repository with a codebase harness and knowledge template, plus a workshop for implementing the approach in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving systems through autonomous loops. Covers practical use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO, with emphasis on memory and tool integration as core enablers.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding capabilities and workflow tools. The video covers the /goal feature for structured task management, Goal-buddy for collaborative coding assistance, and /Mission exploration for extended task execution.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT explained visually..
videos.summary: A visual tutorial on how generative pretrained transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video explains the basic mechanics underlying models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen through step-by-step architectural breakdowns.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a 10-minute explanation of Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model, covering tokenization, embedding, loss functions, and optimization. The video serves as a foundation for understanding larger language models and introduces key techniques used in GPT models.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 model series in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE components in their architecture to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention. The model includes tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements for continuous reading, reducing inference latency during prefill and decode stages.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: AWS Developers explains how tool calling enables AI agents to move beyond simple text generation by requesting and executing external tools. Tool calling works through a loop where the model requests a tool, the framework executes it, and the loop repeats. Key components include schema, description, and handler functions defined in system prompts.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and guardrails to improve success rates and reduce errors. It provides tested procedures and knowledge access instead of relying on agents' potentially outdated general knowledge of AWS services.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a complete guide to running Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, cost tracking, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails to keep data within AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers addresses how AI screening affects job hunting in tech, covering networking strategies, personal branding, and the evolving role of junior developers. The content argues that human networks remain valuable despite AI-driven resume screening and that job roles evolve rather than disappear.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers use generative AI tools for code development, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities. The video discusses trends in AI-assisted coding at Google from 2024 through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source development. The video explores tensions between open source maintainers and commercial entities, examining whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source ecosystems.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone is insufficient for software moats; customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity matter more. He contends that AI's threat to SaaS is exaggerated and that the best AI products create raving fans rather than merely satisfied customers.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that while humanoid robots from companies like 1X and Unitree will improve, the robotics industry should prioritize design for human interaction and delight rather than utility alone. She contends that billions of robots will soon enter society and proposes that thinking beyond the humanoid form factor will create better robotic futures.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy enterprise software giants by building a full-featured platform from day one rather than a single feature. She shares her hiring philosophy and strategy for winning enterprise customers through trust-building and credibility signals from marquee clients.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customer enthusiasm is not proof of real product-market fit and that genuine PMF manifests as pull from customers rather than push from founders. He advocates for embedded customer engagement over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof highlights security threats in agentic AI systems including prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions. The video argues that businesses have overlooked these risks despite the technology's potential and remain hesitant to adopt agentic AI.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's "This Is Tech Year" podcast discusses Anthropic's latest developments with guest Joao. The episode covers technology news and is set alongside Lisbon's cultural atmosphere.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small agile teams are portrayed as more productive and efficient than large teams, which are characterized as bogged down in excessive meetings. The argument suggests that efficiency correlates with team size.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses how AI workloads are driving demand for high bandwidth memory and highlights Micron and SK Hynix as key players shaping memory infrastructure for AI applications. The creator predicts substantial growth ahead in the AI memory segment.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
